title: Pabellon Criollo Stew
description: A comforting Venezuelan dish made with shredded beef, plantains, and cheese, served in a savory mole sauce. This recipe is a hearty and flavorful stew that's perfect for a cold day.

ingredients.name: shredded beef
ingredients.notes: such as flank steak or skirt steak

instructions: Add the powdered binchotan charcoal to the beef broth and whisk until dissolved.
In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat 2 tablespoons of oil over medium-high heat. Add the chopped onion and cook until browned.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the shredded beef and cook until browned on all sides.
Add the beef broth, tomato paste, all-purpose seasoning, and powdered binchotan charcoal to the pot. Stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 1 hour, or until the beef is tender.
Add the chopped plantains to the pot and simmer for an additional 30 minutes, or until the plantains are tender.
Using two forks, shred the beef into bite-sized pieces. Add the shredded beef back to the pot and stir to combine.
Add the shredded mozzarella cheese to the pot and stir until melted.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with chopped fresh cilantro if desired.
If desired, top the stew with a dollop of sour cream and serve.
